title: Relative clauses
explanation:
Relative clauses

We use who for people, which for things, where for places, and whose for possession. We often use relative clauses to add information: The woman who lives next door is a doctor.

questions.text: The man [*who|which|where] helped me was very kind.
This is the book [*which|who|whose] I told you about.
That is the house [*where|who|whose] I grew up.
She is the writer [*whose|which|where] novels have won prizes.
The café [*which|who|whose] opened last month is already popular.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: I know someone ____ can repair your laptop. | *who | which | where
This is the park ____ we first met. | *where | who | whose
The company ____ products I buy is based in Sweden. | *whose | where | which
The film ____ we saw last night was disappointing. | *which | who | where
Do you remember the teacher [*who taught|which taught|where taught] us maths?
The office [*where she works|who she works|whose she works] is in the city centre.
I met a girl [*whose brother|which brother|where brother] is a pilot.
The phone [*which I bought|who I bought|where I bought] was expensive.
That is the restaurant [*where they got married|which they got married|whose they got married] .
